# UC-22.53.22 · AWIA IT / OT Segmentation — detect direct corporate-IT-to-OT traffic that violates the documented segmentation policy and internet-exposed SCADA / HMI / engineering workstations

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Security, Compliance, Operational &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*The water-plant control systems should never be reachable from the office network — and especially not from the internet. We watch all traffic and ring the bell the moment a corporate computer or someone on the internet tries to reach the plant equipment.*

---

## Description

Detects IT/OT cross-zone flows that lack an allow-list entry and any flow involving a non-RFC1918 (public) address adjacent to OT zones.

## Value

Direct internet exposure of SCADA, HMI, or engineering workstations is the leading published cause of water-sector intrusions per CISA. This UC continuously catches both internal segmentation violations and external exposure.

## Implementation

(1) Onboard firewall / NetFlow / Zeek wire data. (2) Curate `awia_segmentation_allow_list_lookup`. (3) Onboard external exposure scanner. (4) Schedule UC-22.53.22 every 5 minutes; severity=critical. (5) Archive every event.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.53.22` archived every 5 minutes to `audit_evidence` with `awia_clause="AWIA-EPA-network-segmentation"`. Every row pins (pwsid, src_ip, dst_ip, src_zone, dst_zone, port, protocol, app, bytes, internet_exposure_detected, internet_exposure_via, segmentation_status).

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Workstation in `corporate_it` zone initiates TCP/502 (Modbus) to `ot_scada` zone. UC-22.53.22 MUST surface within 5 minutes with `segmentation_status="unauthorised_it_ot_flow"`.

### Negative scenario

Historian replication via documented DMZ pathway. UC-22.53.22 MUST record `segmentation_status="allowed"`.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- UC-22.53.8 deployed.
- Splunk ES, Splunk Stream.
- Indexes `index=netflow`, `index=firewall`, `index=zeek`, `index=audit_evidence`.
- Lookup `awia_segmentation_allow_list_lookup`.
- Document the CIDR-to-zone mapping in the search; refresh on any IP-plan change.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Onboard firewall logs at every IT/OT boundary. Onboard Splunk Stream / Zeek to capture protocol detail (Modbus TCP/502, DNP3, IEC-61850, EtherNet/IP, OPC UA). Onboard external-exposure scanner.

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Every 5 minutes.

```ini
[UC-22.53.22: AWIA IT/OT Segmentation]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -5m@m
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.53.22,reg=AWIA,clause=AWIA-EPA-network-segmentation
action.notable.param.severity = critical
alert.severity = 5
alert.suppress = 1
alert.suppress.period = 30m
alert.suppress.fields = pwsid,src_ip,dst_ip,port
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Internal violation**: simulate IT-to-OT Modbus; UC fires.

**(b) Allow-listed**: same flow with allow row; UC clears.

**(c) External exposure**: simulate inbound from public IP to OT zone; UC fires.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

Dashboard `AWIA — Segmentation`. Runbook: shut down the flow; investigate the source; tighten firewall.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**AWIA-specific failure modes**:

- **Cellular-direct PLC**: a remote lift station with cellular PLC may bypass the corporate firewall; ensure cellular APN routes through a managed gateway with the same segmentation rules.
- **Plant management VLAN bleed**: a plant office switch that bridges plant-floor and corporate-IT VLANs is a common silent failure; cross-reference with CMDB to identify dual-purpose switches.
- **OT vendor reverse-tunnel**: CISA water-sector advisories repeatedly cite vendor reverse-tunnels (e.g. TeamViewer / AnyDesk) as the internet-exposure vector; pair this UC with an outbound-tool denylist.
- **Data-diode bypass**: if a data diode is in place but a side-channel exists (e.g. shared USB key), document and disable.

## SPL

```spl
(index=netflow OR index=firewall OR index=zeek) earliest=-5m@m
| eval src_zone = case(
    cidrmatch("10.10.0.0/16", src_ip), "corporate_it",
    cidrmatch("10.20.0.0/16", src_ip), "ot_dmz",
    cidrmatch("10.30.0.0/16", src_ip), "ot_scada",
    cidrmatch("10.40.0.0/16", src_ip), "ot_plc_rtu",
    cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12", src_ip), "plant_floor",
    1=1, "unknown")
| eval dst_zone = case(
    cidrmatch("10.10.0.0/16", dst_ip), "corporate_it",
    cidrmatch("10.20.0.0/16", dst_ip), "ot_dmz",
    cidrmatch("10.30.0.0/16", dst_ip), "ot_scada",
    cidrmatch("10.40.0.0/16", dst_ip), "ot_plc_rtu",
    cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12", dst_ip), "plant_floor",
    1=1, "unknown")
| where (src_zone IN ("corporate_it","unknown") AND dst_zone IN ("ot_scada","ot_plc_rtu","plant_floor")) OR
        (dst_zone IN ("corporate_it","unknown") AND src_zone IN ("ot_scada","ot_plc_rtu","plant_floor"))
| lookup awia_segmentation_allow_list_lookup src_ip dst_ip port OUTPUT allow_reason allow_artefact_url
| eval segmentation_status = if(isnotnull(allow_reason), "allowed", "unauthorised_it_ot_flow")
| eval internet_exposure_via = case(
    src_ip=dst_ip, null(),
    NOT cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8", src_ip) AND NOT cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12", src_ip) AND NOT cidrmatch("192.168.0.0/16", src_ip), "public_to_ot",
    NOT cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8", dst_ip) AND NOT cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12", dst_ip) AND NOT cidrmatch("192.168.0.0/16", dst_ip), "ot_to_public",
    1=1, null())
| eval internet_exposure_detected = if(isnotnull(internet_exposure_via), "true", "false")
| where segmentation_status="unauthorised_it_ot_flow" OR internet_exposure_detected="true"
| table pwsid src_ip src_zone dst_ip dst_zone port protocol app bytes internet_exposure_detected internet_exposure_via segmentation_status
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Live cross-zone flow feed. (Panel 2) Internet-exposure heatmap. (Panel 3) Top denied src/dst pairs.

## Known False Positives

1. **Allow-listed cross-zone flow** — reason and artefact captured.

2. **Documented historian replication** — DMZ-mediated.

3. **Vendor support window** — short-lived allow exception with expiry.

**Suppression**: `awia_22_53_22_exceptions`; max 4h; Network Lead + OT Cybersecurity Lead joint sign-off.

## References

- [EPA / CISA — Network Segmentation](https://www.cisa.gov/water)
- [ISA / IEC 62443-3-3 segmentation requirements](https://www.isa.org/standards-and-publications/isa-standards/isa-iec-62443-series-of-standards)
- [CISA — Water Sector Cyber Intrusion Advisories](https://www.cisa.gov/water)
